specs.reason: Core foundation for socket, HTTP, and urllib implementations. Directly cited in pre-clarifications Q1, Q2, Q4. Provides socket primitives (AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM/DGRAM, bind, listen, accept, connect, send, recv, close) required as foundation for #658 selectors (Q1). Defines HTTP client (HTTPConnection, HTTPSConnection, request, getresponse, read) foundation for #661 SSL wrapper (Q2) and #662 urllib full module (Q4). Spec states: 'socket.socket(AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM) creates TCP/IPv4 socket' (R1), 'HTTPConnection supports timeout parameter' (R4), 'urlopen returns response-like object with read(), status, headers' (R6). Non-goal explicitly states 'Full urllib (parse, robotparser, error hierarchy)' — will be extended by native-implementations pattern. Essential for understanding baseline socket and HTTP implementation.

Foundation for threading and subprocess primitives required by multiprocessing and concurrent.futures. Directly cited in pre-clarifications Q3. Provides threading_mod.rs foundation for Thread, Lock, Event (R1-R4) required as base for #665 concurrent.futures ThreadPoolExecutor (Q3). Provides subprocess_mod.rs foundation for Popen, run, PIPE (R6-R7) required as base for #664 multiprocessing Process/Pool and #665 concurrent.futures ProcessPoolExecutor (Q3). Spec states: 'Thread.start() raises RuntimeError if called more than once' (R2), 'subprocess.run accepts list of strings or single string with shell=True' (R5). Non-goal states 'multiprocessing module (separate spec)' — this spec provides only foundation. Essential for understanding thread pool and process pool backends.

Provides general integration pattern for native stdlib modules including error mapping and symbol registration. Directly supports #661 SSL (Q2), #662 urllib (Q4), and #663 email (all issues). R2 (Module Rewrite) defines pattern for replacing manual implementations with Rust crate-backed versions (rustls for SSL, reqwest/tokio for urllib, lettre for email). R4 (Error Mapping) defines unified error mapping layer converting Rust crate errors (e.g., rusqlite::Error, std::io::Error) into Mamba exceptions with appropriate types and messages — directly applicable to #661 (SSL errors), #662 (HTTP errors), #663 (email errors). R3 (Refined Symbol Registration) addresses module registration lifecycle required for all new modules. Spec provides: 'convert Rust crate errors into MbException' — pattern needed for all native stdlib integration. Supports implementation pattern when stdlib/network.md foundation is extended to full SSL/urllib modules.

Foundation for async runtime integration enabling selectors and high-performance I/O. Directly cited in pre-clarifications Q1. Provides async runtime integration with Orbit event loop (R2: mb_coroutine_suspend/resume, asyncio.run, asyncio.gather) required as foundation for #658 selectors (Q1). R3 (GIL-safe scheduling) ensures async I/O does not block runtime. R4 (Future interoperability with Tokio) enables wrapping Tokio futures for non-blocking I/O — directly required for Q1 'tokio integration for selectors async I/O multiplexing.' Spec states: 'mb_coroutine_suspend() parks coroutine, registers waker with Orbit' (R2), 'GIL is released during coroutine suspension and re-acquired upon resumption' (R3), 'wrap Tokio Future as awaitable Mamba object' (R4). Also supports #665 concurrent.futures ProcessPoolExecutor via async executor (R2: asyncio.gather for concurrent scheduling). Essential for understanding how selectors and async operations integrate with Mamba runtime.

Provides module system architecture and import handling for registering new stdlib modules. R1 (Module System & Imports) defines import aliases and relative imports (R1.1-R1.3) needed for stdlib module imports. Spec states: 'module graph resolution to support multi-file builds' and 'symbol registration' — required infrastructure for registering #658-#665 modules in symbol table and making them importable. Provides only architectural context — actual implementation details are in network.md, concurrency.md, native-implementations.md, and async.md. Low relevance because module system itself is not specific to stdlib-io-networking; same module system applies to all stdlib modules. Value is discovery of relevant import/registration infrastructure only.

notes: REVISION SUMMARY: Fixed PENDING verdict by correcting missing spec and improving Key Requirements accuracy. (1) ADDED stdlib/native-implementations.md (MEDIUM): This spec was referenced in original coverage analysis (line 27: '#663 email: General stdlib pattern, reference stdlib/native-implementations.md') but was MISSING from the specs table. Now added with complete reason explaining how R2 (Module Rewrite), R3 (Symbol Registration), and R4 (Error Mapping) support #661, #662, #663 implementations. (2) REVISED Key Requirements for all specs: Original descriptions conflated question references (Q1-Q4) with actual spec scope. Updated to be more precise and descriptive of what each spec actually provides: stdlib/network.md now explicitly lists 'Socket primitives (AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM/DGRAM...)' instead of vague 'Q1 (socket foundation)'. (3) IMPROVED Issues → Specs Mapping: Each of the 6 issues (#658-#665) now explicitly mapped to multiple supporting specs. Example: '#662 urllib now shows 'Covered by stdlib/network.md (socket, HTTP, and simplified urllib.request foundation) + stdlib/native-implementations.md (native integration pattern for URL parsing and error handling)' instead of single-spec reference. (4) IMPROVED Pre-Clarifications Coverage: Now explicitly shows how each Q (Q1-Q4) is addressed by multiple specs with specific requirement references. Example: 'Q1 (Selectors): runtime/async.md covers async runtime integration with Orbit/Tokio (mb_coroutine_suspend/resume, GIL-safe scheduling); stdlib/network.md provides socket foundation for tokio::net.' (5) REORDERED relevance levels: runtime/async.md is now HIGH (was MEDIUM) to reflect its critical importance for Q1 selectors foundation. all-mamba-p0.md is now LOW (was MEDIUM) to reflect that module system is supporting infrastructure, not domain-specific to stdlib-io-networking. Coverage: All 6 issues (#658-#665) now have explicit multi-spec coverage. All pre-clarifications (Q1-Q4) now have detailed spec support explanation. All specs listed in coverage analysis are now present in the specs table.
